question:
Will Melbourne City FC win on 2025-10-25?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for October 25, 2025
If Melbourne City FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.a-league.com.au/

evidences.title: A-League Men 25-26 Season Guide: Tickets, fixtures, how to watch
evidences.content: ## What are the key season dates? The Isuzu UTE A-League 2025-26 season kicks off on the weekend of October 17-19. In 2025-26, the Isuzu UTE A-League will feature 12 teams following the announcement Western United’s Club Participation Agreement (CPA) has been placed in conditional hibernation for the upcoming campaign. The 26-round, 12-team campaign sees each club play 22 home-and-away fixtures, and will meet four teams on three occasions throughout the season. FULL FIXTURES: Every match of the Isuzu UTE A-League 25-26 season here #### Get ready for Round 1 Round 1 is headlined by an F3 Derby between Central Coast Mariners and Mark Milligan’s Newcastle Jets on Sunday afternoon, after the season gets underway with two enticing fixtures on Friday night. evidences.title: A-League Season Guide: Favourites, storylines, biggest names
evidences.content: Facebook X WhatsApp LinkedIn ) 6 ) ### Summary football360.com.au answers all the biggest A-League questions ahead of season 2025-26 kicking off on October 17. Who should be title favourites? Which coaches are under pressure? What is every team’s biggest storyline? Plus, what impact will the newly launched Australian Championship have on the top flight. ## When does the A-League season kick off? The 2025-26 A-League Men season is scheduled to kick off on Friday, 17 October, 2025 with Adelaide United hosting Sydney FC at Coopers Stadium and will run until the Grand Final on the weekend of 22-24 May, 2026. ## Who should be favourites? [...] ## Who are the young stars to watch? The A-League is brimming with young Australian and Kiwi talent with designs on becoming the next star to emerge and earn a big move abroad, particularly given the hype around the latest batch of exports led by Nestory Irankunda (Watford) and Adrian Segecic (Portsmouth). Clubs are trusting their academy stars more than ever before and almost every team has a teen talent eyeing a breakthrough campaign in 2025-26. Click here for a full breakdown of the Class of 2026. ## What were the most consequential transfer...

evidences.title: Season predictions: Expert tips for A-League Men 2025-26
evidences.content: ## Erik Paartalu’s season predictions Erik Paartalu won two Isuzu UTE A-League Championships and one Premiership with Brisbane Roar and made 119 league fixtures across stints at Roar and Melbourne City. Brisbane Roar great and Network 10 football expert Erik Paartalu has predicted Auckland FC to go one better than their Premiership triumph in 2024-25, and win the Championship in their second season in the Isuzu UTE A-League. In a swap of last season’s trophy winners, Paartalu has predicted reigning Champions Melbourne City to clinch the Premiership, while failing to turn their first-place finish into a trophy double. [...] CLICK HERE TO BECOME AN A-LEAGUES CLUB MEMBER TODAY! CLICK HERE TO VIEW THE FULL 2025-26 FIXTURE ##### Paartalu’s predictions Champions: Auckland FC Premiers: Melbourne City Wooden Spoon: Central Coast Mariners Golden Boot: Kosta Barbarouses (Western Sydney Wanderers) Johnny Warren Medal: Max Burgess (Newcastle Jets) Surprise package: Newcastle Jets Breakout star: Paul Okon-Engstler (Sydney FC) ## Roy O’Donovan’s season predictions Roy O’Donovan scored 56 goals in 128 Isuzu UTE A-League games across stints at Central Coast Mariners, Newcastle Jets and Brisbane...

evidences.content: Melbourne City Home News # TEAM NEWS: Squad confirmed for Home Opener Staff Writers 23 October, 2025 ## Share ### Share this link via ### Share this: ### Or Copy Link Melbourne City has finalised our extended squad for Saturday night’s Isuzu UTE A-League home opener at AAMI Park against Perth Glory. Aurelio Vidmar’s side will enter the clash full of confidence following a dramatic come-from-behind win in the AFC Champions League Elite on Tuesday night, with late goals from Elbasan Rashani and Max Caputo sealing City’s first three points of the continental campaign.
